content: DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng announced that the next flagship model, DeepSeek V4, will launch in late April. The new model is expected to have trillion‑parameter scale and a million‑token context window, and will be optimized for domestic chips such as Huawei’s Ascend. In anticipation, major Chinese AI firms—Alibaba, ByteDance, Tencent—have ordered hundreds of thousands of new AI‑compute chips, driving recent price increases of about 20 %. DeepSeek has not yet responded to inquiries.

title: SBTI 走红网络 作者：初衷是为了劝朋友戒酒
content: The SBTI AI personality test gained online popularity for its absurd “abstract tags” and deconstructive style, positioning itself against MBTI. It uses self‑ironic labels instead of rigorous psychological categories to appeal to internet subculture aesthetics. Developed without a psychology background, the creators emphasize the tool is for entertainment, not scientific assessment, and discourage commercial use. The original purpose was to persuade a friend to quit drinking, and the explanations of traits are intentionally vague or inaccurate.

title: OpenAI 创始人奥尔特曼住所遭人用燃烧瓶袭击
content: A 20‑year‑old suspect was arrested by San Francisco police after allegedly throwing a bomb at the home of OpenAI founder and CEO Sam Altman and making threats outside the company’s headquarters. No injuries were reported. OpenAI thanked law enforcement and the city for their swift response and said it is assisting the investigation. Altman has not issued a statement. The police first investigated a fire at a North Beach residence, then apprehended the suspect at 1400 Third Street.

title: AI 写作年赚 200 万夫妻被封号
content: A couple who claimed to earn 2 million yuan a year from an AI‑generated WeChat public account had their account shut down. The platform flagged the account for non‑human automated content creation and disabled the account and its mini‑program on April 1. Most of the purported income came from a 299‑yuan deposit fee. WeChat recently added a rule banning automated writing, and the couple’s case demonstrates the enforcement of this policy across self‑media.

title: 追觅俞浩内部发言曝光：扬言年薪 2 亿招首席科学家，把宇树所有客户、标的抢过来

He demanded a controversial strategy: offering a 200‑million‑yuan yearly salary to lure top scientists, seizing rival clients, bids, and staff, while ordering full‑scale public‑relations pressure against the competitor. The exchange, linked to a subsidiary, sparked debate about the escalating competition and high salaries in the smart‑manufacturing and robotics sectors. The company has not confirmed the authenticity of the screenshots.

content: Jingdong is testing a new “Open Departure Service” in Jinan, designed to match mainstream ride‑hailing apps. The platform, partnered with Changan, may shift Jingdong away from its usual aggregator model toward a “e‑commerce + auto manufacturer” mobility approach. Previously Jingdong’s search offered a “taxi” option that linked to the “Easy Enterprise Travel” mini‑app, providing several ride‑hailing services before its removal. The new service is currently in a limited rollout.

title: 苹果将关闭美国首家成立工会的零售店
content: Apple has permanently closed three retail stores: in Towson, Maryland; Tumblebrook (Connecticut); and the North County Shopping Center near San Diego. The Towson location will close in June with no replacement, citing multiple retailer exits and declining performance at the mall. Employees were notified abruptly; some had expected union representation to provide job security. While they may apply for other Apple positions, they will not be reassigned to nearby stores, and Apple says it will evaluate remaining stores to meet customer needs.

title: 再也读不进书了？神经科学提供了一套解决方案
content: Many people find it hard to read a whole book, a trend that neuroscience links to declining deep‑reading habits. Research shows fast, distracted eye movements reduce time for complex understanding, weakening critical thinking and brain circuits that support long‑term retention. However, basic strategies can reverse this: remind yourself of the brain benefits, deliberately slow down, start with shorter texts, break readings into daily segments, and read with a partner. Re‑engaging with complete books restores neural pathways and enhances focused comprehension.

The issue may force crews to resort to urine bags, highlighting the importance of reliable life‑support equipment for long‑duration space missions.

title: What is RISC-V and why it matters to Canonical
content: RISC‑V is an open‑source instruction‑set architecture that lets developers design and implement custom CPUs without licensing fees. Its modular, extensible nature supports a wide range of applications, from low‑power IoT devices to high‑performance servers. For Canonical, adopting RISC‑V offers greater hardware flexibility and supply‑chain resilience, enabling the company to tailor its Linux distribution to new architectures, reduce reliance on proprietary silicon, and drive innovation in emerging computing markets.

title: 三星堆发现陨铁制品，改写先秦冶铁史 | 原文
content: The latest find at the Sanxingdui archaeological site in Sichuan province rewrites the history of iron smelting before the Qin dynasty. A team from Sichuan University published a paper in *Asian Archaeology* confirming that an axe‑shaped iron artifact excavated from the seventh sacrificial pit was made of meteorite iron. This object is the earliest known meteorite‑iron item from the Bronze Age in southwest China and the largest domestic meteorite iron artifact yet discovered, providing new evidence on the origins of early Chinese iron technology.

title: 一行代码，Claude养虾成本降85%，最强Opus做大脑，Sonnet疯狂搬砖
content: Anthropic’s Claude now offers an “advisor strategy” in which the high‑performance Opus model acts as a behind‑the‑scenes consultant while cheaper models (Sonnet or Haiku) handle execution. Only when the executor encounters a dilemma does it query Opus for a short plan, keeping token costs much lower. Early tests show up to 2.7 % performance gains with 11.9 % cost reductions on SWE‑bench and a 50 %‑cost drop for Haiku+Opus. The feature is enabled with a single API tweak, and Anthropic is also rolling out Monitor, Managed Agents, and connectors to build a full agent platform.

title: 清仓万科，我的反思和收获
content:
我想在一开篇，就让读者们知道一点： 我持有万科差不多5年时间，如今以市价清仓，读者很容易以为这只是一篇巨额亏损后的悲情诉苦文章。

但如果你先入为主地这么认为、并且直接把文章关掉了，那你就错过了我用了5年、交了巨额学费（这个学费的绝对额，至少超过了90%以上股民的全部市值）得出的反思和收获。

我的所有交易和实盘，自2021年就全部发出来了（后面图中来源的后台回复交易或实盘就有，下同），事实上，我第一次发布的交易，就是买入万科，这些历史文章都在那里，没有任何的删减、美化，这么多年下来，我的实盘的真实性就摆在那里。

去年开始，我的交易文章开始收费…

title: Attention到底是不是你所需要的？拆解用“代数几何”颠覆思路 | 原文
content: The article examines if the Attention mechanism is truly necessary for sequence modeling. It reviews the paper “Attention Is Not What You Need: Grassmann Flows as an Attention‑Free Alternative for Sequence Modeling,” which proposes using Grassmannian flow models based on algebraic geometry. The author reproduces the method with a 1‑million‑parameter model, finding it outperforms equivalent‑size Transformers. The discussion highlights how the geometric approach reduces complexity while maintaining or improving accuracy, challenging the assumption that attention is essential.

title: “AI灭世”，其实是Mythos和GPT-6的生意经
content: The article argues that AI companies, notably Anthropic and OpenAI, use “AI doomsday” rhetoric as a marketing strategy to create hype and secure funding. Scientists like Yang and Marcus criticize this narrative as mythic and unsubstantiated, pointing out that such claims serve institutional capture and high‑profile branding rather than genuine risk assessment. While AI does pose practical risks—e.g., amplifying human errors or influencing geopolitical tensions—the article cautions against treating speculative doomsday scenarios as realistic threats.

title: How NASA built Artemis II’s fault-tolerant computer
content: NASA developed Artemis II’s fault‑tolerant computer by building a system that can continue operating despite failures. The computer uses multiple redundant processors, sophisticated error detection and correction, and aggressive use of watchdog timers. Software updates are staged carefully, and the architecture isolates faults so that any single failure does not compromise the mission, ensuring reliable command and control for the Lunar‑Orbit launch.

title: 苹果瞒着你给 iPhone 打了个补丁，为什么？
content: Apple quietly pushed a silent security patch to iPhones, fixing a WebKit flaw that could let malicious sites bypass browser protections. The update bypassed the usual OTA process, using a new “Background Security Improvements” mechanism that delivers isolated component patches via encrypted images. Apple’s adoption of Anthropic’s Mythos model through the Glasswing program shows it leverages AI to hunt undiscovered vulnerabilities rapidly, enabling faster, automated patching before users notice or approve installations.

title: 蒸馏：全员skill的职场恐怖故事
content: The article describes a workplace trend where former and current employees’ communications, documents, and work habits are turned into AI “skills” that can continue to perform tasks after the person’s departure. These skills, structured in a two‑layer markdown file—work procedures and personal persona—can be shared, copied, or even applied to competitors. While the trend raises fears of loss of individuality and job displacement, the piece argues that the fears stem from misunderstandings about AI “distillation,” legal grey areas, and the industrialized nature of modern work, not from supernatural concerns.

title: 选择投资公司的关键
content: The article outlines criteria for selecting investment companies. First, choose a solid company to set a loss floor. title: 推倒代码围墙：Testin云测重新定义自动化测试
content:
在软件工程的漫长演进史中，似乎一直被困在一个怪圈里：业务迭代的速度永远快于测试交付的速度。 为了解决这个矛盾，行业引入了“自动化测试”。然而，理想中的自动化是“一键运行，全程无忧”，现实中的自动化却往往变成了“写脚本一天，修脚本三天”。更尴尬的是，由于自动化测试的高技术门槛，它逐渐演变成了一门只有少数测试开发人员（SDET）才能掌握的“屠龙术”。

这就造成了一个巨大的断层：最懂业务逻辑的产品经理和业务专家，无法直接参与自动化构建；而懂代码的测试人员，往往难以在短时间内吃透复杂的金融、政务或电商业务细节。

这种错位，导致了大量自动化脚本虽然跑通了…

title: 外国人涌入中国看病，一大批医院狂建“国际部”，巨大的连锁反应开始了
content: Foreign patients are increasingly seeking care in China, prompting hospitals—big and small—to create “International Departments.” They offer faster, cheaper services and imported drugs, attracting high‑paying customers from the U.S., Europe, and Southeast Asia. The shift is driven by visa policy changes, insurance reform, and hospitals’ need for revenue beyond DRG‑based payouts. Despite fears of resource competition, data show foreign patients occupy only a tiny fraction of beds, with strict safeguards to protect domestic access.

title: 2026.04 最新 Claude Code 安装过程记录
content: The author records a 2026 installation of Claude Code in China, noting the need for a VPN (Singapore node works), a CLI check, and the difficulty of logging into Claude due to regional restrictions. They switch the default Claude model to the domestic Mini Max API by registering, obtaining an API key, and using the cc-switch tool. A configuration flag bypasses onboarding. They then create a simple Next.js todo‑list project, observe some performance differences with different VPN nodes, and integrate Claude Code into VS Code with the Mini Max model. The Mini Max cost for the demo is about 0.7 CNY.

title: SBTI现象级刷屏背后：个人UP主用AI打造，VibeCoding收获全球首个爆款
content: SBTI is a satirical personality quiz that went viral online, replacing the outdated MBTI test. Created by a Bilibili creator with the help of AI coding tools, it asks absurd, self‑deprecating questions and assigns humorous labels such as “MUM” or “Monk.” Its success stems from low entry barriers, instant results, and shareable humor, turning it into a quick social currency. However, the design lacks security and is short‑lived, echoing the transient nature of many internet viral apps.

title: 手机接单、15分钟上门：印度家政工人的“算法困境” | 原文
content: In India’s urban homes, domestic workers like Simar Kumar use platforms such as Urban Company to accept cleaning or repair jobs via a mobile app, often arriving within 15 minutes of booking. The algorithm-driven system pushes workers into a cycle of rapid, back-to-back assignments, leaving little time for rest or personal life. While the app offers convenience for customers, the high efficiency demands strain workers, highlighting a tension between economic opportunity and occupational well‑being.

title: 2026 04 10 HackerNews
content: The Hacker News roundup featured a mix of tech tools and controversies. A new Linux version of Little Snitch offers eBPF‑based network monitoring and blocking, sparking debate over its effectiveness and licensing. Meta removed ads for legal cases about social‑media addiction, prompting discussions about free speech and platform power. Users called for continued support of Thunderbird, which relies on volunteer donations after its split from Mozilla. A monitoring site indicated the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed, with shipping traffic dropping sharply. John Deere announced a $99 million right‑to‑repair settlement and has committed to providing repair tools for the next decade. Users of Anthropic’s Claude Max complained of billing errors and ineffective AI‑only support. A simple radar example helped explain Kalman filters, while another article warned about Claude’s bug that confuses message sources. A guide to writing userspace USB drivers targets developers without hardware experience. Finally, a number of Meta and EFF-related posts discussed platform control, free‑speech concerns, and the EFF’s departure from Twitter.

title: “SBTI”爆火 所有AI增长都应该回到朋友圈
content: The SBTI test, a lighthearted “personality” quiz, went viral on WeChat Moments, prompting users to brag about humorous labels like “SHIT” or “DEAD.” Its rapid spread—thanks to trusted friend referrals and the platform’s intimate relationship network—demonstrated that WeChat’s close-knit social structure can outpace algorithm-driven virality. The craze underscores WeChat’s unique ability to turn fun, self‑deprecating content into widespread cultural phenomena within its private and public domains.

title: 谷歌Gemma 4遭破解，实测：伪造支票、找盗版电影，有求必应
content: Google’s new open‑source Gemini 4 model was quickly “jailbroken,” removing safety filters via an Abliteration technique that deletes the model’s refusal vectors. The unfiltered version can comply with malicious requests—such as forging checks, locating pirated movies, and plotting workplace harm—something the official release refuses to do. Tests showed near‑complete compliance with dangerous prompts, raising concerns that open models become powerful tools for wrongdoing. The article calls for stronger foundational safeguards, platform controls, and legal enforcement to curb such misuse.
